records.ety: [LL. deprivare, deprivatium, to divest of office; L. de- + privare to bereave, deprive: cf. OF. depriver. See Private.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deprived (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Depriving.]

defs: To take away; to put an end; to destroy.
To dispossess; to bereave; to divest; to hinder from possessing; to debar; to shut out from; -- with a remoter object, usually preceded by of.
quotes: God hath deprived her of wisdom.
It was seldom that anger deprived him of power over himself.

defs: To divest of office; to depose; to dispossess of dignity, especially ecclesiastical.
